# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent missing vulnerability scan.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build artifacts
        run: npm run build

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to production..."
          # Secure deployment command here
          curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $DEPLOY_TOKEN" https://api.yourservice.com/deploy

  security_scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Run vulnerability scan
        run: |
          echo "Running vulnerability scan..."
          # Secure vulnerability scanning command here
          npm audit --production --audit-level=high
